experiment: Prompt ablation: effect of texture count instruction on inference mIoU

original_1to6.prompt: This image contains exactly 1 to 6 main visually distinct regions...

exact_2.prompt: This image contains exactly 2 main visually distinct regions...

few_open.prompt: This image contains a few main visually distinct regions...

conclusions: Counter-intuitive: forcing correct k=2 REDUCES mIoU by -0.035 (0.660 → 0.625)
The k_pred=6 over-generation is NOT the main problem
Hungarian matching at eval rescues k=6: it picks the best 2 of 6 channels, which are better than the 2 produced when forced
Open-ended 'a few' prompt: Qwen naturally predicts k=2 for 84% of samples, but mIoU is still lower
The bottleneck is EMBEDDING QUALITY, not count. More descriptions = more chances for good embeddings
Implication: don't constrain Qwen's count. Instead improve the quality of its top-K embeddings
